_README: Cloud Routine template for hv: auto-clean a feature when its PR merges. Fires once per merge (event-driven = low cost), runs on Anthropic infrastructure, needs no live session. Fill in <owner>/<repo>; the exact field names depend on your Claude Code / Cloud Routines version, so reconcile against the routine-creation UI/CLI before installing. The point that does not change: a GitHub pull_request trigger filtered to merged PRs runs the command `/hv:clean-agents <branch>`.
name: hv: cleanup on PR merge
description: When an hv feature PR is merged, reclaim its background agent, worktree, and branch via /hv:clean-agents.
notes: Install once at the repo level and every merged hv PR cleans up automatically — no per-feature setup.
clean-agents delegates the destructive work to the janitor subagent, which re-checks that the agent is idle and the PR merged before removing anything.
{{pull_request.head.ref}} is the merged branch (feat/<id>); adjust the templating token to your routine engine's syntax.
